title: Type Certificate Revitalization
text: Preface

title: Type Certificate Revitalization
text:
Executive Summary
marenco-aviation GmbH, led by Martin Stucki, the engineer behind the innovative design and development of the Marenco Swisshelicopter SH09 (now AW09), proposes to acquire the Type Certificate (TC) and production equipment for the twin-engine MD900 helicopter.
This initiative aims to upgrade the avionics, re-establish the supply chain, and bring this proven platform back into production to address a significant gap in the FAR/CS27 certified twin-helicopter market.
The project leverages Marenco’s existing engineering and design capabilities, a well-defined market need, and the potential for early revenue generation through spare parts for the existing MD900 fleet.

title: Type Certificate Revitalization
text:
Opportunity

Market Gap
A clear need exists for a modern, efficient twin-engine helicopter in the FAR/CS27 class. The Airbus EC135 and H145 currently dominate, but operators seek alternatives that offer the right balance of size, performance, and cost.


MD900 Strengths
The MD900 boasts a large cabin, low noise levels (thanks to its NOTAR system), and a smooth ride due to its five-bladed rotor system. These features made it popular for HEMS, police, and VIP transport.


Untapped Potential
The existing MD900 fleet is eager for spare parts and upgrades, presenting an immediate revenue opportunity. A modernized cockpit and improved avionics can significantly enhance the helicopter’s appeal.


Competitive Advantages

Opportunity to deliver a twin-engine helicopter with the lowest vibration and noise level in its class.
One of the largest cabins.
No tail rotor as a safety feature, especially in confined landing spots and operation close to people.
Modernized product at an attractive price

title: Type Certificate Revitalization
text:
Strategy
1.  Acquire the TC: Secure the Type Certificate and tooling for the MD900.
2.  Upgrade and Modernize: Integrate modern avionics and a user-friendly cockpit.
3.  Re-establish Supply Chain: Build a reliable network of suppliers in the US and Switzerland.
4.  Production: Establish assembly lines in both the US (Mesa, Arizona) and Switzerland (Mollis).
5.  Support Existing Fleet: Provide spare parts and maintenance services to current MD900 operators.
6.  Future Development: Explore upgrades such as a shrouded tail rotor, single-engine variant, and fuselage extensions.

title: Type Certificate Revitalization
text:
Competitive Landscape

Airbus: Dominates the market with the EC135 and H145. The upcoming H140 variant demonstrates the continued demand for this class of helicopter.
Bell: The 429 suffers from high noise levels and limited payload.
Leonardo: The A109 remains competitive due to its attractive price and high speed, despite its smaller cabin.

title: 1  Introduction
text:
1.2 Critical Components for Serial Production

1.2.1 Gearbox
The needs to be maintained and manufactured with a new partner. This shall be done together with Sauter, Bachmann AG in Netstal. For the testing we need to build a test-bench. It will be similar to the one we built for the SH09 project, only with two input motors instead of one.


1.2.2 Flexbeams
For the flexbeams of the rotor-head, we will work in parallel with the existing supplier and with Carbo-Link AG in Fehraltorf. Carbo-link is specialised in carbon tension cables.


1.2.3 Rotorblades
Also parallel work with Carbo-link and the existing supplier. Carbo-link designed and delivered two different rotorblades for P2 and for the Elbit Systems program together with Marenco AG.


1.2.4 Tension-Torsion Strap (“TT Strap”)
TT Strap of the fan for the Notar shall be replaced with the design as it is used nowadays in Fenestrons (“Fan-in-Fin”-Tail Rotors).


1.2.5 Integrated Instrument Display System (“IIDS”)
The actual IIDS design can not be used any longer. Different suppliers can supply the same functionality on a multi function display.


1.2.6 Autopilot
We will evaluate between three existing suppliers: Genesys, Garmin, and Thales.


1.2.7 Fuselage
The fuselage has many parts, so early in the project we must define how they will be manufactured, assembled, and whether a partner will produce the entire fuselage.

title: 3  Products and Services
text:
3.1 Description of your products and services
The MD900 was produced between 1995 and 1997. It is expected that the existing fleet still stands at 85 units.
The MD902 Explorer was produced between 1997 and 2022. It is expected that the existing fleet still stands at approximately 135 units.
Fleet holders would profit from a new supply of spare parts and upgrades.
Hence, revenues can be generated from:

title: 3  Products and Services
text:
Spare parts sales for the existing fleet.
Maintenance and upgrade services.
New helicopter sales.

title: 4  Financial Projections
text:
4.1 Inflation
The following cash flow model estimates the annual price inflation for costs and personnel to be 1,5%.
The price inflation for helicopters sold, however, is at 3.5% for the first 3 years, and is reduced to 2% after the market entry of marenco-aviation GmbH, because of the changing market dynamics.

title: 4  Financial Projections
text:
4.5 Company Valuation

4.5.1 Corporate Tax
As of 1st January 2024 Switzerland introduced the OECD minimum taxation for international companies of 15% for consolidated revenues of the group of more than EUR 750 Million per year.1 As EUR differs from the base currency of the calculations CHF, the accumulated turnover over any 12 months will be converted into Swiss Francs at the current EURCHF rate. At this point of the model, we do not add any fluctuations in the foreign exchange rate for this purpose.
There is a great likelihood that the hurdle annual consolidated revenues may be reached, and the actual corporate tax rate of 12.6% (see facts and figures on the website of Kontaktstelle Wirtschaft, Kanton Glarus) will be increased to 15% towards the end of our cash flow model.
We assume that taxes are paid in the same month as they occur. There is no treatment of pre- or post-payment of taxes, nor any interest earned or paid taxes being paid earlier or later.


4.5.2 Company Value over Time
For the visualisation of the company value over thime we assumed:

The above tax considerations, with a EURCHF exchange rate of 0.94
a discount rate of the free cash flow for the first 33 months of 12%, and 8% thereafter
a tax loss carry forward of a maximum of 7 years.

title: 5  Acquisition Process of TC
section: 5.2 Previous Letters to Seabury Securities LLC
text:
5.2 Previous Letters to Seabury Securities LLC
In addition to the ongoing telephone conversations with the sell-side investment bank, marenco-aviation GmbH sent:
an initial non-binding LOI on April 15, 2025, and
clarifications to the LOI on April 24, 2025.
Seabury Security LLC expects a binding LOI to move forward.
